translations.en: a man dressed in velvets and silks of yellow, green and blue.
translations.zh: 一位身著黃、綠、藍三色天鵝絨與絲綢的男子。

translations.en: His voice is rich and mellow.
He glances behind him and ducks, as if expecting someone to throw something at him.
translations.zh: 他往身後瞟了一眼，隨即低頭躲閃，彷彿預期有人要往他身上扔什麼東西。

translations.en: "Bit of a brawl going on just now. Be glad to talk to you some other time."
translations.zh: 「現在正鬧著一場鬥毆。改天再與汝暢談。」

translations.en: "How do you do. I am the bard Van Kellian."
translations.zh: 「幸會。吾乃吟遊詩人范凱利安。」

translations.en: "Please, speak your name, $G."
"A pleasure, to be sure, $Z."
"A very good $T to ye, my friend."
"Call me Van."
"Why, singing is my trade."
"Songs to soothe the soul and refresh the mind."
"Shall I sing for ye?"
Iolo rolls his eyes and whispers to you.
translations.zh: 尤洛翻了個白眼，在汝耳邊低語。

translations.en: "He believes he does us a favor. This I must hear."
translations.zh: 「他以為自己是在幫我們。這個我非聽不可。」

translations.en: "Well? How about a @song?"
"'Tis such a topsy-turvy world
When my sweet lady-love whirls by
I would that I could catch her eye
translations.zh: 吾但願能引她回眸一盼

translations.en: Sing ho eyo he hum!"
"Did ye know that the mantra of pride is 'mul?' A @beggar told me that."
translations.zh: 「汝可知驕傲之真言為『mul』？一位@beggar（乞丐）告訴吾的。」
translations.note: mantra 'mul' stays as game mechanic word

translations.en: "There is little enough dignity in the life of a beggar."
translations.zh: 「乞丐的生活中所剩無幾的尊嚴。」

translations.en: "I gave him a few coins, of course."
"Starhelm and I have often debated the nature of the gargoyle @Sin 'Vraal."
translations.zh: 「史塔赫姆與吾曾多次辯論魔像@Sin（辛弗）拉的本質。」

translations.en: "If Lord British suffers him to live, then I will not harm him."
translations.zh: 「若不列顛王容他存活，吾便不傷害他。」

translations.en: "What possible threat could one @gargoyle living alone in the Dry Lands be?"
translations.zh: 「一個獨居於旱地的@gargoyle（魔像族），能有何威脅？」

translations.en: Stelnar says "I'll tell ye, Van."
translations.zh: 史塔赫姆說：「吾來告訴汝，范。」

translations.en: "If ye let one gargoyle live,
then ye'll be tempted to spare trolls,
translations.zh: 則汝便會心軟放過食人魔，

translations.en: and then even cyclopses."
"And then ye forget valour, and soon ye are overrun by monsters!"
translations.zh: 「然後汝忘卻勇氣，很快便被怪物淹沒！」

translations.en: "Valour comes from the courage to be virtuous."
translations.zh: 「勇氣來自於踐行美德的勇敢。」

translations.en: "Just as compassion comes from a love of all mankind."
"Aha! That means monsters are not deserving of mercy!" says Stelnar
translations.zh: 「啊哈！那就是說怪物不值得憐憫！」史塔赫姆說道

translations.en: "Courage grows. Why not love?"
"Well, I hear their skin is stony and rough."
translations.zh: 「嗯，吾聽說牠們的皮膚粗糙如石。」

translations.en: "Their cries are fierce yowlings to make the blood run cold with fear."
translations.zh: 「牠們的嚎叫猛烈刺耳，令人聞之膽寒。」

translations.en: "Though, mind ye, I myself have never faced one in combat."
translations.zh: 「不過，請注意，吾本人從未親自與之交戰。」

translations.en: "Good $T, and farewell."
"Hmm. No, I cannot help ye with that."
"Well, perhaps another time, then."
The bard plays a harp and sings.
translations.zh: 詩人撥弄豎琴高聲吟唱。

translations.en: "Tho' she disdains to speak to me
Ah how I pine and sigh for her
I would that I were handsome- o
Sing ho eyo he hum!"

translator_notes: 范凱利安是吟遊詩人，語氣雅致柔軟，與史塔赫姆的粗獷形成對比。歌詞力求韻律感，以意境為主。真言『mul』保留英文。尤洛的旁白調侃范的演唱天賦。
